entry.title: Atmospheric Palimpsest: Sulfur as the Key to Exoplanet Interiors
entry.summary: Joint modeling of planetary evolution and chemical kinetics has shown that although the bulk atmosphere of rocky exoplanets inherits the composition of gases released from the mantle, the star's photochemistry radically alters the upper layers. Yet sulfur dioxide (SO₂) accumulates there to concentra

entry.title: Icy Origins: Most Hot Jupiters Come from the Cold
entry.summary: Scientists simulated planet formation in a protoplanetary disk, tracing the path of carbon and oxygen across the snow lines of water and carbon dioxide. Comparison with real spectra of hot Jupiters showed that most began life in the outer cold of the disk and then underwent violent migration. This d

entry.title: How Galactic Dust Traps Heat from Invisible Black Holes
entry.summary: Primordial black holes are elegant dark matter candidates. Their Hawking radiation heats cosmic dust, offering a new way to assess their fraction. For the first time, spin and halo profiles are systematically accounted for: rotation dramatically boosts heating, and the isothermal profile sets a limi

entry.title: The Cosmic Score is Silent: Dark Matter and Neutrinos Don't Play a Duet
entry.summary: Comparing data from Keck and VLT telescopes with model spectra from hydrodynamic simulations, scientists used a neural network emulator and Bayesian analysis. The upper limit uνχ ≤ 1.5×10⁻⁸ is a hundred times stricter than previously allowed. Dark matter and neutrinos barely notice each other: they

entry.title: The Milky Way Symphony: Why Star Clocks Tick at Different Speeds
entry.summary: To circumvent the challenge of directly determining stellar ages, astronomers turned to 'chemical clocks'—pairs of elements whose ratio changes over time. Yttrium and magnesium are among the most promising pairs: magnesium disperses rapidly after supernova explosions, while yttrium slowly accumulate

entry.title: Shadow Symphony: How Data Became a Portrait of a Black Hole
entry.summary: In 2019, the Event Horizon Telescope collaboration unveiled the shadow of the black hole at the center of M87—an image that became a scientific icon. Yet it wasn't a direct photograph: two years were spent transforming sparse signals from eight antennas into a meaningful picture. Every step—from cal

entry.title: Spin from Bend: How Curvature Twists Sound
entry.summary: In flat crystalline membranes, flexural vibrations do not feel the phonon Hall viscosity—a peculiar, non-dissipative 'fluid' property of media with broken time-reversal symmetry. However, static curvature translates vertical motion into in-plane shear deformation, unlocking the hidden Hall response.

entry.title: Cement's Boson Hum: Terahertz Waves Eavesdrop on Nano-Inhomogeneities
entry.summary: Scientists have experimentally discovered the boson peak — a universal anomaly in the vibrational spectrum of disordered media — in calcium silicate hydrate (C-S-H), the main binding phase of hardened cement. Using terahertz spectroscopy and low-temperature heat capacity measurements, they showed th

entry.title: Cosmic Strings in a Nanocomposite: How to See the Echo of the Big Bang
entry.summary: In a recently published study, scientists obtained images of spontaneously arising magnetic vortices in a material where superconducting MgB₂ grains are randomly scattered in a dielectric MgO matrix. Unlike compact Abrikosov vortices, these structures stretch several micrometers and have a bizarrely

entry.title: Requiem of a Torn Star: Infrared Excess and the Birth of a Corona
entry.summary: Just 40 days after a supermassive black hole tore apart a star in event 2024aepd, infrared telescopes detected an unexpected excess of radiation. Its flat spectrum pointed not to a dust echo but to free-free emission from an extended plasma envelope. Meanwhile, X-ray observations captured the rapid

entry.title: How a Gravitational Trampoline Saves Dust Grains from a Black Hole
entry.summary: A study of random walks of dust particles in curved spacetime revealed a paradox: the probability of falling into a black hole is lower than in flat space. Geometric drift — the stretching of radial cells — and time dilation flatten the accretion power spectrum at low frequencies, matching observati

entry.title: Strongly Mixed Worlds: Inflation as a Cosmic Collider
entry.summary: A new mathematical framework for two-field inflationary models with powerful mixing allows precise calculation of the CMB non-Gaussianity, bypassing perturbation theory approximations. Summing effects to all orders, the authors found closed-form expressions for three-point correlators, revealing exp

entry.title: Reading the Cosmic Palimpsest: Redshift Tomography of Unresolved Galaxies
entry.summary: A new study proposes a method for estimating the redshift distribution of unresolved galaxies directly from multiband maps of the cosmic optical background. Using conditional normalizing flows trained on realistic image simulations from Euclid and LSST, the model achieves sub-percent accuracy. The m

entry.title: Invisible Gravity: The Dance of Double Stars Challenges Newton
entry.summary: Three independent samples of binary stars from Gaia data underwent unprecedented scrutiny. But neither the removal of false close encounters nor accounting for hidden companions eliminated the anomaly: at large distances, stars move faster than prescribed by the law of universal gravitation. The dev

entry.title: The Planet Still: LHS 1140b's Helium Exhale
entry.summary: For the first time, variable helium leakage has been detected from the rocky planet LHS 1140b in the habitable zone of a red dwarf. The WINERED spectrograph on the Magellan telescope captured a signal revealing hydrodynamic outflow with a minuscule amount of hydrogen (0.1%). Models depict a distilla

entry.title: Photon Fog Forging Antimatter
entry.summary: Astrophysicists have simulated how often gamma-ray photons from the cosmic photon background (CPB) smash into each other and give birth to electron-positron pairs. Breaking down the CPB into a blend of blackbody spectra and tracking how sources evolved with redshift, they spotted a baby boom—roughly

entry.title: A Top on the Abyss's Edge: How Polarized Light Betrays Black Hole Spin
entry.summary: A black hole's spin is a fundamental parameter that determines not only the geometry of spacetime but also the energy of its jets and accretion disks. Polarization images from the Event Horizon Telescope revealed three channels of spin influence: photon trajectories in curved space, boundary conditi

entry.title: Geoglyph Calendars: How the Amazon Catches the Sun
entry.summary: A lone researcher deciphered the language of geoglyphs from space photos. Three non-random orientation peaks coincide with astronomical events, turning the Amazonian earthworks into a world-class archaeoastronomical phenomenon. The discovery forces us to reconsider the region's image: not a 'green d

entry.title: Super-magnified stars: How ripples on the light ocean reveal dark matter's secrets
entry.summary: Massive galaxy clusters act as giant gravitational magnifying glasses, amplifying the light of individual stars thousands of times. But on small scales, dark matter behaves differently. Like ripples on a water's surface, it shatters the lens's caustic network into microscopic filaments. When a super

entry.title: The Shadow Theater of Black Holes: One Shadow, Two Plays
entry.summary: Dark matter or hidden dimensions? Both hypotheses can cast an almost identical silhouette. By tweaking parameters, scientists obtained indistinguishable shadow sizes, but it’s not a dead end: the finest details — the photon ring, its shape and polarization — promise to reveal the true nature of grav

entry.title: Symphony of a Neutron Star: AI Agent Plays the Score of Dense Matter
entry.summary: Introducing NNStar, an AI agent that builds a nuclear matter model from scratch, solves equations, derives an equation of state, and performs Bayesian analysis—all without human intervention. Validation on classic models and successful optimization of an extended Lagrangian confirm the machine’s hig

entry.title: Reflation: When a Planet Inhales After Exhaling
entry.summary: Hot super-Earths continuously shed mass under fierce stellar radiation, but some manage to hold onto a dense envelope. Numerical modeling of interior and atmosphere evolution shows: a reduced (oxygen-poor) mantle first outgases a heavy carbon monoxide atmosphere. When the star strips this layer, hyd

entry.title: Sapphire Tuning Fork: How to Hear the Echo of the Big Bang
entry.summary: A cubic meter of perfect sapphire, cooled near absolute zero, becomes a detector for gigahertz gravitational waves. The wave excites quanta of sound—phonons—in the crystal, and resonant amplification at Van Hove singularities allows us to hear even rare events: the stochastic background from inflati

entry.title: Cosmic Metronomes: The AM CVn Spectral Code for the LISA Era
entry.summary: For the first time, an analytical bridge is built between the X-ray, ultraviolet, and optical emission of AM CVn systems and their key parameters: white dwarf masses, orbital period, and accretion rate. All emitting components—from the accretion disk and boundary layer to the impact spot—are conside

entry.title: Mirror Rift: How Supernova Neutrinos Set Life's Left-Handed Bias
entry.summary: The study proposes a model in which neutrinos from supernovae, interacting with organic molecules in interstellar clouds, create a tiny energetic preference for one of the mirror isomers. This effect is amplified by autocatalytic reactions and stochastic fluctuations, leading to a significant enanti

entry.title: The Chameleon Universe: How Gravity Learns to Hide
entry.summary: Genetic algorithms put the Hu–Sawicki f(R) model to the test, where gravity changes strength with the environment. Comparison with supernovae and baryon acoustic oscillations showed: the surviving function is almost indistinguishable from ΛCDM. The dark energy equation of state wavers within hundred

entry.title: Cosmic Earthquakes: Algorithm Reveals the Rhythm of Gamma-Ray Bursts
entry.summary: Gamma-ray bursts—titanic explosions in the depths of space—produce ultra-relativistic jets, whose kinetic energy converts into radiation through shell collisions. For the first time, this model was directly calibrated against three datasets (BATSE, Swift, Fermi) using a genetic algorithm. The optimi

entry.title: Listening to the Stars: AI Decoder Instantly Reconstructs the Neutron Star Equation of State
entry.summary: Neutron stars are laboratories where matter is compressed to nuclear and supranuclear densities. Their equation of state (EoS) is the central mystery of astrophysics. A new approach based on reversible neural networks with physical regularization allows extracting the EoS directly from observations,

entry.title: Neutrino Rebellion Decoder: How AI Reads Chaos in Stellar Cores
entry.summary: Fast flavor conversions — quantum rebellions of neutrinos in the cores of supernovae and merging neutron stars — alter the course of the explosion and the birth of heavy elements. Scientists have trained machine learning algorithms to predict the onset of this instability from coarse data — densitie

entry.title: The Neutrino Aria of Black Holes: Listening in on Silent Dark Matter
entry.summary: A new study offers an elegant way to hunt for dark matter: listening to the neutrino echo emitted by clouds of light scalar bosons around rotating primordial black holes. Gravitational superradiance – akin to laser amplification – makes these systems radiate an almost monochromatic neutrino flux wit

entry.title: The Turbulent Conductor: How the Blazhko Effect Revealed the Mystery of Stars
entry.summary: The century-old mystery of the flickering of RR Lyrae variable stars has finally been explained. Ultra-precise photometry from the Kepler telescope revealed a group of "deharmonized harmonics," whose antiphase dynamics directly point to plasma boiling at the base of the convective envelope. The disc

entry.title: The Quantum Waltz of Annihilation: What Photons Reveal About Tissues
entry.summary: Modern quantum positron emission tomography (QE-PET) turns polarization entanglement of gamma quanta into diagnostic contrast. Unlike standard PET, QE-PET measures Compton scattering of both photons, extracting the degree of their quantum connection. Experiments have shown that entanglement is not m

entry.title: Hearing the Silence: The Hunt for Scalar Dark Matter
entry.summary: This work opens three windows into the silent music of scalar dark matter. Clumps colliding with comets strike X-ray flares, networks of atomic clocks catch the breathing of fundamental constants, and astrometric microlensing traces the gravitational dance. For the first time, one invisible sector i

entry.title: Space Fireflies: Hunting for the Laser Pulse of Extraterrestrial Civilizations
entry.summary: The interstellar laser beacon hypothesis elegantly solves the Fermi paradox: instead of shouting into the void, intelligent species scatter cheap, long-lived probes that drop periodic microsecond flashes. The 'Space Beacons' project for the first time implements a full-sky survey with 10-microsecond

entry.title: Molecular Melodies: How ELT/ANDES Will Tune Into the Breath of Exoplanets
entry.summary: New modeling on 18 transiting Earth-like exoplanets showed: water vapor is detectable in 10–19 transits, while oxygen requires four times more. These estimates turn the search for biosignatures with ANDES/ELT into a realistic observation plan.

entry.title: Stochastic Tsunamis: Diffuse Scalar Background from Black Hole Formation
entry.summary: This work proposes a new mechanism for generating a relic background of scalar radiation. Massive stars host a static scalar field configuration, which, upon collapse into a black hole, is released as a 'scalar tsunami.' The superposition of many such events over cosmic history creates a stochastic

entry.title: Dark Timbre: How Gravitational Waves Sound the Invisible Halos
entry.summary: A new study proposes a detection method for a population of low-mass cold dark matter halos. They are predicted by cosmological simulations but have so far eluded observation. Passing through unresolvable clouds of dark matter, gravitational waves from black hole mergers experience frequency-depende

entry.title: Star Harp: How Orbits Around a Black Hole Weigh the Graviton
entry.summary: At the heart of the Milky Way, around the black hole Sagittarius A*, stars S2 and S38 trace orbits like strings on a tensed cosmic harp. Astronomers used two independent methods—precession analysis and numerical simulation—to capture an echo of the graviton's mass in this motion. It turned out that

entry.title: Laser Vortex Spawns Axions: When Light Vanishes in Air
entry.summary: In a three-dimensional light vortex, like a tiny tornado, photons momentarily vanish and reappear — this cycle is precisely described by transformation into an axion and back. A 5% dip at the trap’s center is direct evidence of the creation of particles invisible to ordinary detectors, capable of so

entry.title: Neural Network Hears Magnetic Music
entry.summary: Traditional XMCD analysis relies on sum rules, which are vulnerable to background and systematics. In a new study, a neural network predicts ⟨Sz⟩ and ⟨Lz⟩ directly from line shapes, trained on thousands of simulated spectra of Fe, Co, Ni. An R² accuracy > 0.99 paves the way for automatic characteriz

entry.title: Symphony of Stellar Memory: How Chaos Preserves Galactic History
entry.summary: Astronomers created an information-theoretic tool to measure how many 'memories' of past mergers and star formation are preserved in the Milky Way's stellar halo. Using APOGEE spectroscopy and Gaia astrometry, they identified three types of memory: dynamic, chemical, and cross-memory. It turned out

entry.title: Soil Subway: Bacteria's Dance with the Environment in the Tight Squeeze of Pores
entry.summary: Soil is a planetary-scale bioreactor: each gram teems with up to a billion bacteria, orchestrating fertility and climate. Long considered passive tenants, they were revealed through transparent micro-labs to be engaged in a continuous dance of mutual reshaping. This review examines the physics of th

entry.title: Topology by Aroma: How One Spin Gives Away the Quantum Phase
entry.summary: Physicists have developed a method to classify quantum phases using data from just a few sites—including phases where order is hidden from view. It combines a quantum kernel built from reduced density matrices of a small subsystem with a classical support vector machine. Tests on spin chains showed

entry.title: The Cosmic Bell: One Peak Gives Birth to Dark Matter and Gravitational Waves
entry.summary: Buried in the early Universe lies a master key to two great mysteries: dark matter and gravitational waves. New work shows how a single peak in the spectrum of primordial inhomogeneities gives birth to both superheavy dark matter particles and a background hum of gravitational waves at megahertz fre

entry.title: The Liquid Mirror of the Horizon: How a Partonic Ocean Gives Black Holes an Electric Soul
entry.summary: The entropy uncovered by black holes has long demanded a microscopic explanation. A new model depicts the horizon as a Fermi sea of partons on a noncommutative geometry, where topological effects create an effective magnetic field. This horizon is not a silent wall — it acts like a liquid mirror: it

entry.title: Local Big Bang: Explosive Outflows at the Birth of Massive Stars
entry.summary: In dense clusters of newborn stars, protostars don't just grow — they collide and merge, releasing gravitational energy comparable to a supernova explosion. The result is explosive molecular outflows: hundreds of thin gas jets spraying outward isotropically at speeds up to 350 km/s. The most strikin

entry.title: Polymer Flying Carpet: Growth and Bending Set It in Motion
entry.summary: In the microworld, where viscosity dominates over inertia, microorganisms use clever tricks to move. Researchers have developed a mathematical model of an 'active carpet' of growing filaments and shown that compressive forces from polymerization trigger a buckling instability that breaks symmetry an

entry.title: Ghostly Duet: Neutrino Decay of a Superluminal Photon
entry.summary: What if light could cheat its own speed? Gamma rays of extreme energies from the Crab Nebula, registered by the LHAASO observatory, make us wonder: is Lorentz invariance truly unbreakable? Theory allows that in quantum loops a photon can acquire an effective mass and decay into a neutrino pair — an

entry.title: Symphony of Atoms: How AI Turned the Microscope into a Conductor of Matter
entry.summary: Where the eye once saw blurry blobs, convolutional networks now paint an atomic ballet. Detection, segmentation, and 3D reconstruction are near-flawless, liberating scientists from drudgery. But the real breakthrough: algorithms learned to read hidden physics in the pixels—activation energies, diffu

entry.title: Excavations of Reality: Two Modes of Neural Network Learning via the Ising Model
entry.summary: The study revealed how autoencoders, trained on Ising model spin configurations, independently extract macroscopic variables. At first, networks grasp global magnetization, then switch to local energy correlations. Deep models can get 'stuck' halfway if the learning rate is too high. It's like an ar
